description: Fix lint warnings and lint-staged inefficiency discovered during platform contributor experience QA testing in Docker

userStories.title: Fix react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in side panel App.tsx
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want `npm run lint` to produce zero warnings so that real warnings aren't hidden by persistent noise
acceptanceCriteria: `npm run lint` produces 0 errors and 0 warnings (currently shows 2 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings)
The `loadPlugins` function at line 61 and `handleSearchChange` function at line 102 no longer cause useEffect dependency warnings at line 265
Side panel behavior is unchanged — plugin loading, search, and notification handling still work
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx`

The `loadPlugins` function (line 61) and `handleSearchChange` function (line 102) are defined as regular functions inside the component body, recreated on every render. They are listed as dependencies in the `useEffect` at line 265, causing the exhaustive-deps warning because they change on every render.

The CLAUDE.md says React Compiler handles memoization automatically, so do NOT use `useCallback` or `useMemo`. Instead, restructure the code:

1. Move the `loadPlugins` function definition INSIDE the useEffect callback at line 265. The function already accesses refs (lastFetchRef, pendingTabStates) and setters (setPlugins, etc.) which are stable, so moving it inside is safe.

2. For `handleSearchChange` — the useEffect listener needs it because it handles `ws:message` events that trigger search. If it can be moved inside the effect, do so. If it must remain outside (because it's also used by JSX event handlers), convert the useEffect to use a ref-based approach: store the latest `handleSearchChange` in a ref, and call the ref from the listener.

The key constraint: the CLAUDE.md says 'Do not use useMemo, useCallback, or React.memo for optimization — React Compiler handles memoization automatically.' So find a structural solution, not a memoization one.

userStories.title: Split lint-staged config to avoid running eslint on JSON files
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want pre-commit hooks to run only relevant tools on each file type so that commits are faster
acceptanceCriteria: The lint-staged config in `package.json` runs `eslint --fix` only on `*.{ts,tsx}` files, not on `*.json` files
The lint-staged config still runs `prettier --write` on `*.{ts,tsx,json}` files
The lint-staged config still runs `prettier --write` on `*.md` files
Pre-commit hook still works: staging a `.ts` file runs both prettier and eslint, staging a `.json` file runs only prettier
